title: Logarithm
short: The inverse of an exponent: log_b(x) is the power b must be raised to in order to get x.
full:
A logarithm answers 'what exponent?'. If b^y = x, then log_b(x) = y. So log₂(8) = 3 because 2³ = 8. The base b is a positive number other than 1; the natural log ln uses base e, and 'log' on its own usually means base 10.

Because logarithms are the inverse of exponentials, they turn multiplication into addition: the laws of logarithms split a log of a product, quotient or power into sums, differences and multiples. That is what makes them the standard tool for solving exponential equations.

The domain of log_b(x) is x > 0 — you can never take the log of zero or a negative number.
